title: Missing width in assignment to typedefed wildcard
body:
Defining wildcard type in a typedef, and then using the type can cause problems with missing widths on nodes.

```
typedef string dict_t [*];
dict_t b = '{default: "nope", "BBBBB": "fooing", 23'h434343: "baring"};
```

Results in:
```
%Error: Internal Error: t/t_assoc_wildcard.v:27:21: ../V3Width.cpp:6237: Under node SETWILDCARD has no expected width?? Missing Visitor func?
                                                  : ... In instance t
-node: SETWILDCARD 0x105f9e0 <e2618#> {e27av} @dt=0x1032ea0@(str)[*]
   27 |          dict_t b = '{default: "nope", "BBBBB": "fooing", 23'h434343: "baring"};
      |                     ^~
```

The error comes from [this](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/11948987/t_assoc_wildcard.zip) modification of an existing test.

Tested on 71df9e3381fae2ee0fa75cd3de3664998f089dff from last Sunday.
code_blocks:
%Error: Internal Error: t/t_assoc_wildcard.v:27:21: ../V3Width.cpp:6237: Under node SETWILDCARD has no expected width?? Missing Visitor func?
                                                  : ... In instance t
-node: SETWILDCARD 0x105f9e0 <e2618#> {e27av} @dt=0x1032ea0@(str)[*]
   27 |          dict_t b = '{default: "nope", "BBBBB": "fooing", 23'h434343: "baring"};
      |                     ^~
